content.description:
Mobile-specific views and navigation rather than responsive desktop UI.

## Approach

Don't make the desktop UI responsive. Instead, create mobile-specific containers and views:

- Same underlying data (notes, tags, tasks)
- Different layout views (linear scroll vs spatial canvas)
- Platform detection selects appropriate container

## Mobile-Specific Views

- **Note mobile view**: Full-screen, swipeable
- **Container mobile view**: Vertical scroll, no spatial layout
- **Tag browser mobile**: Hierarchical drill-down instead of tree view

## Design Questions

- Separate entry point or same URL with platform detection?
- What subset of features makes sense on mobile?
- Is editing on mobile important, or primarily consumption?
- What's the offline storage limit on mobile browsers?

## Implementation Notes

The item system supports this naturally — views are items, and different views can render the same data differently. A mobile viewport view could be a separate view item that presents a touch-optimized interface.

## Priority

Medium — depends on whether [Collaboration & Sync](item://c0c0c0c0-0044-0000-0000-000000000000) is implemented first (mobile without sync is limited to single-device use).
